---
title: "Mouse Forecast"
subtitle: "Current season"
description: "House mouse abundance across Australian grain-growing regions"
title-block-banner: "#EBEBEB"
title-block-banner-color: "black"
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
    theme: cosmo
    fontcolor: black
    fig-width: 8
    fig-asp: 0.8
css: mouse_update_raw_data.css
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
editor: source
toc: TRUE
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

## Load packages
library(targets)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(lubridate)
library(sf)
library(terra)
library(stringr)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggpattern)   # hatched polygon overlays
library(ggnewscale)  # second fill scale for farmer-report layer

## Load targets — handle both pipeline rendering (from project root) and
## standalone rendering (from inside quarto_reports/)
store_path <- if (dir.exists("_targets")) "_targets" else "../_targets"
targets::tar_load(c(data_filtered, aez_adj, aus_shp), store = store_path)

## Load anecdotal high-abundance farmer-report polygons.
## Path resolves whether rendering from the project root or quarto_reports/.
anecdotal_path <- if (file.exists("raw_data/anecdotal_data/high_abundance_reports.gpkg")) {
  "raw_data/anecdotal_data/high_abundance_reports.gpkg"
} else {
  "../raw_data/anecdotal_data/high_abundance_reports.gpkg"
}
high_abundance_reports <- sf::st_read(anecdotal_path, layer = "polygons", quiet = TRUE)

## Town / city labels for map orientation (used as landmarks)
town_labels <- sf::st_as_sf(
  read.csv(if (file.exists("raw_data/town_labels.csv")) "raw_data/town_labels.csv"
           else "../raw_data/town_labels.csv"),
  coords = c("longitude", "latitude"), crs = "EPSG:4326", remove = FALSE
)


## Set default ggplot theme
theme_update(
  panel.grid      = element_blank(),
  legend.position = "bottom"
)

## Shared colour palette for Low / Moderate / High / No data
cat_colours <- c(
  "Low"     = "#91cf60",  # green
  "Moderate"  = "#ffeda0",  # yellow
  "High"    = "#f03b20",  # red
  "No data" = "grey80"
)
```

```{r}
#| label: attach_aez
#| include: false

## Attach ae_zone to each survey dataset via spatial extraction.
## ae_zone is not present in data_filtered (added later in the pipeline via
## data_add_aez), so we replicate that step here using the aez_adj shapefile.
attach_aez <- function(df, aez) {
  df_vect <- terra::vect(sf::st_as_sf(df, coords = c("longitude", "latitude"), crs = "epsg:4326"))
  x       <- terra::extract(terra::vect(aez), df_vect)
  # Sites that fall just outside the shapefile boundary default to NSW N Qld S
  df$ae_zone <- as.factor(ifelse(is.na(x$ae_zone), "NSW N Qld S", x$ae_zone))
  df
}

data_traps     <- data_filtered$traps     |> attach_aez(aez_adj)
data_burrows   <- data_filtered$burrows   |> attach_aez(aez_adj)
data_chewcards <- data_filtered$chewcards |> attach_aez(aez_adj)
```

```{r}
#| label: seasons
#| include: false

## Identify the most recent and second-most-recent seasons with any survey data.
## season_year_adj is an ordered factor — its levels give the chronological order.
all_levels <- levels(data_traps$season_year_adj)

seasons_with_data <- union(
  union(
    as.character(na.omit(data_traps$season_year_adj)),
    as.character(na.omit(data_burrows$season_year_adj))
  ),
  as.character(na.omit(data_chewcards$season_year_adj))
)

seasons_ordered  <- all_levels[all_levels %in% seasons_with_data]
latest_season    <- tail(seasons_ordered, 1)
prev_season      <- if (length(seasons_ordered) >= 2) tail(seasons_ordered, 2)[1] else NA_character_
prev_prev_season <- if (length(seasons_ordered) >= 3) tail(seasons_ordered, 3)[1] else NA_character_
```

```{r}
#| label: set_subtitle
#| results: asis
#| include: true

## Dynamically replace the "Current season" subtitle placeholder with the
## current month and year at render time. Quarto renders the subtitle as a
## <p class="subtitle"> element inside the title block; we update its text via
## JavaScript at load time. This is the most reliable approach for dynamic
## subtitles in self-contained HTML.
cat(sprintf(
  '<script>
    document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", function () {
      var el = document.querySelector("#title-block-header p.subtitle");
      if (!el) el = document.querySelector("p.subtitle");
      if (el) el.textContent = "%s";
    });
  </script>',
  format(Sys.Date(), "%B %Y")
))
```

```{r}
#| label: site_metrics
#| include: false

## ── Detection status per site × season ───────────────────────────────────────
## For each method, a site is "detected" in a season if at least one visit
## recorded evidence of mice.  The three method-level results are then combined:
## a site is considered detected if ANY method found mice that season.

detect_burrows <- data_burrows |>
  filter(!is.na(burrow_transects_searched), burrow_transects_searched > 0) |>
  group_by(ae_zone, region, site, subsite, season_year_adj) |>
  summarise(detected = any(burrow_total_count > 0, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

detect_chewcards <- data_chewcards |>
  filter(!is.na(chewcards_deployed), chewcards_deployed > 0) |>
  group_by(ae_zone, region, site, subsite, season_year_adj) |>
  summarise(detected = any(chewcards_chewed > 0, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

detect_traps <- data_traps |>
  filter(!is.na(number_mice_caught), !is.na(number_functional_traps),
         number_functional_traps > 0) |>
  group_by(ae_zone, region, site, subsite, season_year_adj) |>
  summarise(detected = any(number_mice_caught > 0, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

## Combine methods: detected = TRUE if any method found mice at that site-season
site_detection <- bind_rows(detect_burrows, detect_chewcards, detect_traps) |>
  group_by(ae_zone, region, site, subsite, season_year_adj) |>
  summarise(detected = any(detected), .groups = "drop")
```

```{r}
#| label: aggregate_spatial
#| include: false

## Aggregate site detection up to each spatial grouping level.
## prop_detected = proportion of surveyed sites that detected mice this season.

aggregate_detection <- function(group_cols) {
  site_detection |>
    group_by(across(all_of(c(group_cols, "season_year_adj")))) |>
    summarise(
      n_sites      = n_distinct(paste0(region, site, subsite)),
      n_detected   = sum(detected, na.rm = TRUE),
      prop_detected = n_detected / n_sites,
      .groups = "drop"
    )
}

hist_aez    <- aggregate_detection("ae_zone")
# Group by region only (not ae_zone + region) so that regions whose sites
# happen to straddle two AE zones are not split into duplicate rows.
hist_region <- aggregate_detection("region")
```

```{r}
#| label: combined_score
#| include: false

## ── Categorise proportion detected into Low / Moderate / High ──────────────────
##
## prop_detected (0–1) is the share of surveyed sites that detected mice via
## any method.  Absolute thirds are used as thresholds:
##   Low    < 1/3 of sites detected
##   Moderate  1/3 – 2/3 of sites detected
##   High   > 2/3 of sites detected

categorise_prop <- function(df) {
  df |>
    mutate(
      cat_combined = factor(
        case_when(
          is.na(prop_detected)      ~ "No data",
          prop_detected < 1 / 3     ~ "Low",
          prop_detected < 2 / 3     ~ "Moderate",
          TRUE                      ~ "High"
        ),
        levels = c("Low", "Moderate", "High", "No data")
      )
    )
}
```

```{r}
#| label: best_available
#| include: false

## ── Best-available season summaries ──────────────────────────────────────────
##
## For each zone/region, use the current season if data exist; otherwise fall
## back to the previous season.  An is_current flag (TRUE/FALSE) is carried
## through so the maps can render fallback polygons at reduced opacity and the
## text can flag that some results are from an earlier survey round.
## Regions not surveyed in the past 12 months are excluded entirely.

## Helper: map season-year string to an approximate midpoint date so we can
## identify seasons that fall within the most recent 12 months.
parse_season_date <- function(sy) {
  parts  <- strsplit(as.character(sy), "-")
  months <- c(Summer = 1L, Autumn = 4L, Winter = 7L, Spring = 10L)
  dates  <- sapply(parts, function(p) {
    as.Date(paste(p[2], months[p[1]], "15", sep = "-"))
  })
  as.Date(dates, origin = "1970-01-01")
}

## Seasons whose approximate midpoint falls within the past 12 months.
cutoff_date    <- Sys.Date() - lubridate::years(1)
recent_seasons <- seasons_ordered[parse_season_date(seasons_ordered) >= cutoff_date]

## Regions surveyed at least once in the past 12 months (used to filter the map)
recent_regions <- hist_region |>
  filter(as.character(season_year_adj) %in% recent_seasons) |>
  select(region) |>
  distinct()

## Helper: for each spatial unit in `hist`, return the best available
## activity category — current season first, then up to two older seasons as
## fallbacks for units that still have no data.
## Adds two extra columns:
##   is_current  – TRUE if data come from the latest season
##   data_season – name of the season the data come from
best_season_data <- function(hist, id_col, latest_s, prev_s, prev_prev_s = NA_character_) {
  current <- hist |>
    filter(as.character(season_year_adj) == latest_s) |>
    select(all_of(id_col), prop_detected) |>
    mutate(data_season = latest_s, is_current = TRUE)

  ## Helper to build a fallback frame, or an empty frame if the season is NA
  make_fallback <- function(s) {
    if (is.na(s)) return(current[0, ])
    hist |>
      filter(as.character(season_year_adj) == s) |>
      select(all_of(id_col), prop_detected) |>
      mutate(data_season = s, is_current = FALSE)
  }

  fallback      <- make_fallback(prev_s)
  fallback2     <- make_fallback(prev_prev_s)

  ## All units ever surveyed
  all_units <- hist |> select(all_of(id_col)) |> distinct()

  all_units |>
    left_join(current,  by = id_col) |>
    ## Suffix each fallback to avoid column name clashes during coalesce
    left_join(fallback  |> rename_with(~paste0(.x, "_fb"),  -all_of(id_col)), by = id_col) |>
    left_join(fallback2 |> rename_with(~paste0(.x, "_fb2"), -all_of(id_col)), by = id_col) |>
    mutate(
      prop_detected = dplyr::coalesce(prop_detected, prop_detected_fb, prop_detected_fb2),
      data_season   = dplyr::coalesce(data_season,   data_season_fb,   data_season_fb2),
      is_current    = dplyr::coalesce(is_current,    is_current_fb,    is_current_fb2),
      ## Units with no data in any season: treat as current (full opacity grey)
      is_current    = dplyr::coalesce(is_current,  TRUE),
      data_season   = dplyr::coalesce(data_season, latest_s)
    ) |>
    select(all_of(id_col), prop_detected, data_season, is_current) |>
    categorise_prop()
}

## AE zone — best available data; all ever-surveyed zones retained
display_aez <- best_season_data(hist_aez, "ae_zone", latest_season, prev_season, prev_prev_season)

## Region — best available data; restricted to recently-surveyed regions
display_region <- best_season_data(hist_region, "region", latest_season, prev_season, prev_prev_season) |>
  dplyr::semi_join(recent_regions, by = "region")

## Add cat_display: a plain Low / Moderate / High / No data factor used by the
## fill scale.  Previous-season polygons are shown with the same colours as
## current-season polygons, so no season suffix is needed in the label.
add_cat_display <- function(df) {
  df |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      cat_display = factor(
        as.character(cat_combined),
        levels = c("Low", "Moderate", "High", "No data")
      )
    )
}

display_aez    <- display_aez    |> add_cat_display()
display_region <- display_region |> add_cat_display()
```

```{r}
#| label: spatial_objects
#| include: false

## ── Prepare spatial objects for each aggregation level ────────────────────────

## AE zone: join display data (including is_current / data_season) to the
## aez_adj polygon.  Filter to zones that have been surveyed at least once.
aez_surveyed <- unique(hist_aez$ae_zone)
aez_map <- aez_adj |>
  filter(ae_zone %in% aez_surveyed) |>
  left_join(display_aez, by = "ae_zone") |>
  ## Zones with no match get full-opacity grey "No data" treatment
  mutate(is_current = coalesce(is_current, TRUE))

## Region: build exactly one polygon per region.
## Strategy: combine all site points for a region, take the convex hull
## (a single polygon regardless of how many clusters exist), then buffer it.
## This guarantees one POLYGON per region — buffering individual points and
## unioning them can produce a MULTIPOLYGON when site clusters are far apart.
## Buffer distance (metres) adds a margin around the outermost sites.
buf_m <- 40000  # 40 km buffer radius

all_site_pts <- bind_rows(
  data_burrows   |> select(ae_zone, region, longitude, latitude),
  data_chewcards |> select(ae_zone, region, longitude, latitude),
  data_traps     |> select(ae_zone, region, longitude, latitude)
) |>
  distinct(ae_zone, region, longitude, latitude) |>
  filter(!is.na(longitude), !is.na(latitude)) |>
  sf::st_as_sf(coords = c("longitude", "latitude"), crs = "EPSG:4326") |>
  # Project to GDA94 Australian Albers (metres) for accurate buffering
  sf::st_transform(crs = "EPSG:3577")

## Group by region only (not ae_zone + region) so regions whose sites span
## two AE zones produce a single polygon rather than one per zone.
region_buffers <- all_site_pts |>
  group_by(region) |>
  summarise(
    # st_combine → single geometry; st_convex_hull → one polygon; st_buffer → margin
    geometry = sf::st_buffer(
      sf::st_convex_hull(sf::st_combine(geometry)),
      dist = buf_m
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  sf::st_transform(crs = "EPSG:4326")

## Join activity scores; use inner_join so that only recently-surveyed regions
## (those in display_region) are retained — regions inactive for > 12 months are
## dropped from the map entirely.  Also compute centroid coords for region labels.
region_map <- region_buffers |>
  dplyr::inner_join(display_region, by = "region") |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    lon = sf::st_coordinates(sf::st_centroid(geometry))[, 1],
    lat = sf::st_coordinates(sf::st_centroid(geometry))[, 2]
  )

## Enforce the full four-level factor on both map objects so that
## scale_fill_manual(drop = FALSE) keeps all legend keys even when some
## categories are absent from the current map.
cat_display_levels <- c("Low", "Moderate", "High", "No data")
aez_map    <- aez_map    |> dplyr::mutate(cat_display = factor(cat_display, levels = cat_display_levels))
region_map <- region_map |> dplyr::mutate(cat_display = factor(cat_display, levels = cat_display_levels))

```

```{r}
#| label: summary_text_prep
#| include: false

## Pre-compute values used in the inline "In short" text.
## Summaries are expressed at the region level (not AE zone level).

## Formatter for AE zone names: apply title case then uppercase the leading
## state abbreviation (e.g. "sa central" → "SA Central", "nsw n qld s" → "NSW N Qld S").
## Uses a Perl back-reference \\U to uppercase the first captured word.
fmt_aez <- function(x) {
  x <- stringr::str_to_title(as.character(x))
  gsub("^([A-Z][a-z]+)(?=\\s|$)", "\\U\\1", x, perl = TRUE)
}

## Helper: format a character vector as natural-language prose.
## fmt is applied to each element before joining (default: title case).
list_names <- function(x, fmt = stringr::str_to_title) {
  x <- fmt(as.character(x))
  if (length(x) == 0) return(NULL)
  if (length(x) == 1) return(x)
  paste(paste(x[-length(x)], collapse = ", "), "and", x[length(x)])
}

## Region-level activity categories
reg_high   <- display_region$region[!is.na(display_region$cat_combined) & display_region$cat_combined == "High"]
reg_med    <- display_region$region[!is.na(display_region$cat_combined) & display_region$cat_combined == "Moderate"]
reg_low    <- display_region$region[!is.na(display_region$cat_combined) & display_region$cat_combined == "Low"]
reg_nodata <- display_region$region[display_region$cat_combined == "No data"]

## AE zone-level activity categories (used in the AE zone figure caption)
aez_high <- display_aez$ae_zone[!is.na(display_aez$cat_combined) & display_aez$cat_combined == "High"]
aez_med  <- display_aez$ae_zone[!is.na(display_aez$cat_combined) & display_aez$cat_combined == "Moderate"]

## Regions / zones whose displayed data come from the previous season
reg_prev_season <- display_region$region[!display_region$is_current]
aez_prev_season <- display_aez$ae_zone[!display_aez$is_current]

n_reg_total <- nrow(display_region)
n_reg_high  <- length(reg_high)
n_reg_data  <- sum(display_region$cat_combined != "No data")

## Build two key-results sentences (high then medium) for use in figure captions.
## unit_label is the singular noun used when no items are present (e.g. "zone").
## fmt is passed through to list_names for name formatting (use fmt_aez for zones).
make_key_results <- function(high_items, med_items, unit_label, fmt = stringr::str_to_title) {
  high_sent <- if (length(high_items) == 0) {
    paste0("No ", unit_label, "s currently show high mouse activity.")
  } else {
    paste0("Monitoring data shows mouse activity to be **high** in ", list_names(high_items, fmt), ".")
  }
  med_sent <- if (length(med_items) == 0) {
    paste0("No ", unit_label, "s currently show moderate mouse activity.")
  } else {
    paste0("Monitoring data shows mouse activity to be **moderate** in ", list_names(med_items, fmt), ".")
  }
  paste(high_sent, med_sent)
}

## Build a suffix note for figure captions when any fallback data are shown.
## The note names the previous season so readers know how old the data are.
## Polygons are shown at full opacity regardless of season.
#make_prev_note <- function(prev_items, prev_s) {
#  if (length(prev_items) == 0 || is.na(prev_s)) return("")
#  paste0(
#    "<br>Some polygons show data from the previous season (",
#    prev_s,
#    ") where current-season surveys are not yet available",
#    " \u2014 *interpret with caution as mouse populations can change",
#    " substantially between seasons*."
#  )
#}

## Pre-compute figure captions.
## The legend-key explanation appears on its own line; a fallback note is
## appended when at least one polygon uses previous-season data.
cap_aez <- paste0(
  "Results of recent mouse monitoring: proportion of sites within each GRDC agro-ecological zone with mice presence (Low = mice detected at 33% of sites or less; Moderate = mice detected between 33 \u2013 67% of sites, High = mice detected at more than 67% of sites).",
  " GRDC agro-ecological zones are broad regions of the Australian grain belt defined by similar climate, soils, and farming systems.",
  " ",
  make_key_results(aez_high, aez_med, "zone", fmt = fmt_aez)
)
cap_region <- paste0(
  "Results of recent mouse monitoring: proportion of sites within each monitoring region with mice presence (Low = mice detected at 33% of sites or less; Moderate = mice detected between 33 \u2013 67% of sites, High = mice detected at more than 67% of sites).",
  " ",
  make_key_results(reg_high, reg_med, "region")
)

```

```{r}
#| label: plot_helpers
#| include: false

## ── Map helper functions ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

## Derive map extent automatically from the surveyed AE zone polygons.
## A small buffer (degrees) is added so polygons aren't clipped at the edges.
map_bbox <- sf::st_bbox(aez_adj |> dplyr::filter(ae_zone %in% aez_surveyed))
map_buf  <- 1.5   # degrees of padding around the data extent
map_xlim <- c(map_bbox["xmin"] - map_buf, map_bbox["xmax"] + map_buf)
map_ylim <- c(map_bbox["ymin"] - map_buf, map_bbox["ymax"] + map_buf)

## Crop town labels to those that fall inside the padded map extent, so we
## only label cities that are visible (avoids clutter outside the grain belt).
town_labels_crop <- dplyr::filter(
  town_labels,
  longitude >= map_xlim[1], longitude <= map_xlim[2],
  latitude  >= map_ylim[1], latitude  <= map_ylim[2]
)

## Build the shared map frame: Australian outline + optional town labels +
## auto-cropped extent.  data_layer is a list of ggplot2 geom / scale objects
## sandwiched between the background and any labels.
## show_towns = TRUE  → add town / city landmark labels (used for AE zone map)
## show_towns = FALSE → suppress town labels (used for region map, which has its
##                      own region-name labels and doesn't need the extra clutter)
build_map <- function(data_layer, show_towns = TRUE) {
  p <- ggplot() +
    geom_sf(data = aus_shp, fill = "white", colour = "grey60", linewidth = 0.3) +
    labs(y = "", x = "") +
    data_layer

  if (show_towns) {
    p <- p +
      geom_sf(data = town_labels_crop, size = 0.75) +
      ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
        data               = town_labels_crop,
        aes(longitude, latitude, label = name),
        size               = 2.5,
        max.overlaps       = 20,
        box.padding        = 0.4,
        min.segment.length = 0   # always draw a line from dot to label
      )
  }

  p +
    coord_sf(xlim = map_xlim, ylim = map_ylim, expand = FALSE) +
    ## Stack multiple legends vertically (activity row first, farmer reports below)
    theme(legend.box = "vertical", legend.box.just = "left")
}

## Colour palette for the four activity categories.
## Previous-season polygons are shown at full opacity (same colours as current
## season), so no faded variants are needed.
base_cols <- c(Low = "#91cf60", Moderate = "#ffeda0", High = "#f03b20")

## Colour lookup: Low / Moderate / High / No data
activity_colours <- c(base_cols, "No data" = "grey80")

## Fill scale for AE zone / region maps.
## cat_display has four levels (Low/Moderate/High/No data); drop = FALSE keeps
## all legend keys even when some categories are absent from the current map.
## has_nodata: pass TRUE only when "No data" polygons are present in the figure.
make_activity_fill <- function(has_nodata = FALSE) {
  shown_levels <- if (has_nodata) cat_display_levels
                  else            setdiff(cat_display_levels, "No data")
  scale_fill_manual(
    values   = activity_colours[shown_levels],
    limits   = shown_levels,
    drop     = FALSE,
    na.value = "grey80",
    name     = "Expected mouse abundance",
    guide    = guide_legend(title.position = "top", nrow = 1L, byrow = TRUE, order = 1)
  )
}

## Farmer-report overlay: white-filled polygons with a diagonal red stripe
## pattern and red border.  ggnewscale::new_scale_fill() resets the fill
## aesthetic so this layer carries its own legend entry ("High - Farmer
## Reports") without clashing with the activity fill scale above it.
farmer_reports_layer <- function() {
  list(
    ggnewscale::new_scale_fill(),
    ggpattern::geom_sf_pattern(
      data            = high_abundance_reports,
      aes(fill        = "High (based on farmer reports)"),
      pattern         = "stripe",
      pattern_colour  = "red",   # hatch line colour
      pattern_fill    = "white", # hatch background
      pattern_angle   = 45,
      pattern_density = 0.3,
      pattern_spacing = 0.025,
      colour          = "red",   # polygon border
      linewidth       = 0.7
    ),
    ## Fill scale for the farmer-report layer: white polygon background,
    ## legend key shows the hatching via override.aes.
    scale_fill_manual(
      values = c("High (based on farmer reports)" = "white"),
      name   = NULL,
      guide  = guide_legend(
        title.position = "top",
        order          = 2,
        override.aes   = list(
          pattern         = "stripe",
          pattern_colour  = "red",
          pattern_fill    = "white",
          colour          = "red",
          pattern_density = 0.3,
          pattern_spacing = 0.025
        )
      )
    )
  )
}

## AE zone map: filled polygons; all legend entries appear because cat_display
## is a complete factor (set in spatial_objects) and drop = FALSE is used.
## The farmer-report overlay is drawn on top via farmer_reports_layer().
plot_aez_map <- function() {
  build_map(list(
    geom_sf(data = aez_map, aes(fill = cat_display),
            colour = "grey50", linewidth = 0.25),
    make_activity_fill(has_nodata = "No data" %in% aez_map$cat_display),
    farmer_reports_layer()
  ))
}

## Region map: as above but with region-name labels and town labels suppressed.
## The farmer-report overlay is drawn on top via farmer_reports_layer().
plot_region_map <- function() {
  build_map(list(
    geom_sf(data = region_map, aes(fill = cat_display),
            colour = "grey50", linewidth = 0.2),
    make_activity_fill(has_nodata = "No data" %in% region_map$cat_display),
    farmer_reports_layer(),
    ## Region labels drawn last so they sit above all polygon layers
    ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
      data               = region_map,
      aes(x = lon, y = lat, label = stringr::str_to_title(region)),
      size               = 2.5,
      max.overlaps       = 30,
      box.padding        = 0.8,
      point.padding      = 0.5,
      min.segment.length = 0.2,
      force              = 2
    )
  ), show_towns = FALSE)
}


```

## Overview

::: {.overview-detail}

### In short
+ [Geraldton, Central Wheatbelt, Esperance (WA): **PREPARE TO ACT AT SOWING**]{style="color: red;"}
+ Adelaide Plains (SA): **WATCH AND ACT AT SOWING**
+ [Southern QLD: **WATCH**]{style="color: grey;"}


### In detail

+ Western Australia: we are receiving concerning reports of very high mouse numbers, suggesting outbreaks could be under way across the (1) Mid West coast around Geraldton, (2) Central Wheatbelt around Merredin, and (3) Esperance region on the south coast (shown by red hatching in the map below). Recent rapid assessment monitoring suggests this likely also extends to the Kwinana West region.

+ Adelaide Plains: strong evidence of moderate-high and increasing population densities from recent survey data.
There is some potential for an outbreak here depending on future conditions.

+ Northern NSW and southern QLD: Generally low activity, although moderate activity seen across Goondiwindi and northern Darling Downs with recent rapid assessment monitoring.

+ Victoria, southern & central west NSW: mouse numbers likely remain low.

:::

## Map

::: {.panel-tabset}

#### Agro-ecological zone

```{r}
#| label: fig_aez
#| fig-cap: !expr cap_aez

plot_aez_map()
```

#### Survey regions

```{r}
#| label: fig_region
#| fig-cap: !expr cap_region

plot_region_map()
```

:::





## Management recomendations

1. Monitor mouse activity by walking through paddocks and [searching for active burrows (chew cards could also be used)](https://grdc.com.au/resources-and-publications/resources/mouse-management/monitor-manage/know-your-mouse-numbers). Two or three active burrows per 100 square metres are cause for concern prior to sowing. 

2. Where you have identified high mouse activity, [consider applying mouse bait](https://grdc.com.au/resources-and-publications/resources/mouse-management/monitor-manage/mouse-baiting) at seeding to prevent damage to the
freshly sown crop. Monitor mouse activity following baiting after application to assess effectiveness of the application. ZnP25 is the only currently registered bait available; use in
accordance with the label instructions and report any adverse effects (including a lack of efficacy) via the [APVMA website](https://www.apvma.gov.au/regulation/adverse-experience-reporting-program). 

3. Reduce available mouse food (e.g., spilt grain) where possible. High rates of background food directly reduces the effectiveness of poison baiting  (as mice are less likely to consume the baits). 




## About

Mouse forecasts are enabled through a [GRDC and CSIRO investment](https://groundcover.grdc.com.au/grdc/announcements/investments-target-new-normal-of-mouse-activity) via the [Rodent Pest Management team](https://research.csiro.au/rm/). 
Forecasts will be updated seasonally or as new data comes available. We are actively developing new statistical models to improve mouse forecasts, and working on their presentation. 

### Monitoring data 
Mouse population surveys are conducted 2 - 3 times a year at each of the ~170 long-term monitoring sites.
Mice are surveyed through active burrow searches and chewcards. To calibrate this data against more robust population density measurements, we  conduct live-trapping at 3 sites (Adelaide Plains SA, Northern Mallee VIC and Central West NSW). 

Mouse monitoring data is collected by CSIRO, NSW Local Land Services, NSW Department of Primary Industries and Rural Development, Central West Farming Services, Birchip Cropping Group, & Farmanco. We particularly thank NSW DPIRD who generously provide additional live-trapping survey data. Further live-trapping data is also occasionally supplied through the [GRDC / CSIRO Mouse Ecology 2024-2029 project](https://research.csiro.au/rm/research-projects/mouse-ecology-2024-2029/).

### Report mice activity 
We also receive reports from farmers, agronomists and other community members. This is key information we consider given we cannot feasibly survey across the entire grain growing region. 

You can quickly report sightings --- and importantly, lack of sightings --- of mice on your farm at [https://feralscan.org.au/mousealert](https://feralscan.org.au/mousealert). 

We also receive reports from the National Mouse Group, a grower-led network of farmers, industry representatives, state government, GRDC and CSIRO. The group meet three times a year to update local reports of the current mouse situation, review management recommendations and determine research priorities. 





## Contact

Seen mice? Want to be involved in the National Mouse Group? Contact Steve Henry [steve.henry@csiro.au](steve.henry@csiro.au)

Questions about the project or forecast? Get in touch with Dr Matthew Rees [matt.rees@csiro.au](matt.rees@csiro.au)

We value your feedback. 

---



::: {.doc-footer}
![](images/logo_nsw_gov.png){height="60px" style="opacity: 0.85;"}

<div>
![](images/logo_csiro.png){height="60px" style="opacity: 0.85;"}
![](images/logo_grdc.png){height="60px" style="opacity: 0.85;"}
</div>
:::
